You are working on beads issue kapow-d1i.1: PostHog client module + test mocks.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/posthog"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main.

## Issue Details

**kapow-d1i.1 — PostHog client module + test mocks** (P1)

Foundation for PostHog integration. Install posthog-js. Create src/lib/posthog.ts following the supabase.ts pattern (graceful degradation when VITE_POSTHOG_KEY is absent). Add global mocks in src/test/setup.ts for posthog-js and posthog-js/react. Document VITE_POSTHOG_KEY and VITE_POSTHOG_HOST in .env.example. Unit test the client module.

**Acceptance Criteria**: posthog-js installed; src/lib/posthog.ts exports posthogEnabled boolean and posthog client; no-op stub when key missing; test mocks prevent side effects; pnpm build && pnpm lint pass

## Plan Reference

Read the plan at docs/plans/kapow-d1i-posthog-integration.md for full context. This issue covers only items 1 (client initialization) and 10 (global mocks) from that plan — NOT the error boundary, hooks, exchange, or provider wiring.

Specifically:
1. Install posthog-js via pnpm
2. Create web/src/lib/posthog.ts — read VITE_POSTHOG_KEY/VITE_POSTHOG_HOST from import.meta.env, export posthogEnabled boolean, init posthog if enabled with the config from the plan, export the posthog instance or a no-op stub
3. Look at web/src/lib/supabase.ts for the pattern to follow
4. Add global mocks in web/src/test/setup.ts for posthog-js and posthog-js/react
5. Document VITE_POSTHOG_KEY and VITE_POSTHOG_HOST in .env.example
6. Write unit tests for the client module
7. Follow all conventions in web/CLAUDE.md and the root CLAUDE.md

## Quality Gate
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test
```
Run this and include the results in your completion message.

When done, push your branch:
```bash
git push -u origin HEAD
```

</teammate-message>

# ─── Database ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PostgreSQL connection string (pgx format)

# ─── Authentication (JWT) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# HS256 shared secret — used when JWT_PUBLIC_KEY is not set (dev / simple clients)
JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret

# RS256 PEM-encoded public key — if set, overrides JWT_SECRET
# JWT_PUBLIC_KEY=-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

# Expected "iss" claim in incoming tokens (optional)
# JWT_ISSUER=https://auth.example.com

# Expected "aud" claims — comma-separated (optional)
# JWT_AUDIENCE=kapow-api

# ── Supabase shortcut ──
# Use the JWT secret from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API → JWT Secret:
# JWT_SECRET=<your-supabase-jwt-secret>
# JWT_ISSUER=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1
# JWT_AUDIENCE=authenticated

# Future: OIDC discovery URL (not yet active)
# OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL=https://auth.example.com/.well-known/openid-configuration

# ─── Supabase (Frontend Auth SDK) ───────────────────────────────────────────
# Project URL and anon key from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API.
# These are used by the Supabase JS SDK in the frontend for login/signup flows.
# SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
# SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...

# Vite exposes these to the browser (VITE_ prefix required)
# VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
# VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...

# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LOG_LEVEL=info          # trace | debug | info | warn | error
LOG_FORMAT=json         # json (default/prod) | text (dev)

# ─── HTTP Debug Logging ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Request header logging: none (off) | selected | all
# HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS=none
# Comma-separated headers to log when mode is "selected"
# HTTP_DEBUG_SELECTED_HEADERS=Content-Type,Accept,User-Agent
# Redact sensitive headers (Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, X-Api-Key)
# HTTP_DEBUG_REDACT_SENSITIVE=true
# Response logging: none (off) | headers | full (headers + body)
# HTTP_DEBUG_RESPONSE_MODE=none
# Max response body bytes to capture when mode is "full"
# HTTP_DEBUG_BODY_MAX_BYTES=4096

# ─── Environment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ENV=development         # development | staging | production

# ─── CORS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Comma-separated allowed origins (empty = no CORS headers set)
CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:8080

# ─── GraphQL ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND=true     # Enable Playground UI (dev only — disable in prod)
GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION=true  # Enable schema introspection

# ─── MCP (Model Context Protocol) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
MCP_ENABLED=true               # Enable MCP server endpoint at /mcp
# MCP requires a valid JWT — same tokens used for /graphql work for /mcp.
# OAuth 2.1 Protected Resource Metadata is served at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource

# ─── OpenTelemetry ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Set to true to enable OTLP HTTP export of traces and metrics.
OTEL_ENABLED=false
# Standard OTel SDK env vars (only used when OTEL_ENABLED=true):
# OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=https://otlp.example.com:4318
# OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS=x-api-key=YOUR_KEY
# OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=kapow-api   (overridden by code — set in Config)
# See https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/configuration/sdk-environment-variables/

# ─── Shutdown ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT=15s

# ─── Export ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MAX_EXPORT_ROWS=500                # Maximum rows in CSV export (default: 500)

# ─── Rate Limiting ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true            # Master switch for all rate limits
RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL=60              # /graphql requests per minute (anonymous)
RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH=120        # /graphql requests per minute (authenticated)
RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT=10               # /api/export/csv requests per minute (per user)
RATE_LIMIT_MCP=120                 # /mcp/* requests per minute (per user)

# ─── Private Endpoint Access ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Comma-separated CIDRs allowed to access /private/* routes
PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS=127.0.0.0/8,::1/128

# PostHog Integration Plan

## Context

Kapow has zero analytics or error tracking. This plan adds PostHog to the web
frontend for error tracking and product analytics, while architecting the
provider hierarchy so session replay, experiments, and feature flags can be
enabled later without structural changes.

## Approach

Follow the existing `supabase.ts` pattern: a library module that gracefully
degrades to no-ops when env vars are missing. Wrap the app with
`PostHogProvider` from `posthog-js/react` so the `usePostHog()` hook and
future feature-flag hooks work throughout the tree.

## New dependency

- `posthog-js` (includes `posthog-js/react` — provider, hooks)

## Environment variables

| Var | Required | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_POSTHOG_KEY` | Yes (to enable) | — |
| `VITE_POSTHOG_HOST` | No | `https://us.i.posthog.com` |

When `VITE_POSTHOG_KEY` is absent, the entire integration is inert.

## Files to create

### 1. `web/src/lib/posthog.ts` — Client initialization

- Read `VITE_POSTHOG_KEY` / `VITE_POSTHOG_HOST` from `import.meta.env`
- Export `posthogEnabled: boolean`
- If enabled: `posthog.init(key, { api_host, capture_pageview: false, capture_pageleave: true, autocapture: false, respect_dnt: true, persistence: 'localStorage' })`
- Register `window.onerror` / `window.onunhandledrejection` → `posthog.capture('$exception', ...)`
- Export the posthog instance
- If disabled: export a no-op proxy (mirrors supabase.ts pattern)

### 2. `web/src/hooks/usePostHog.ts` — Pageview + identify hooks

- `usePostHogPageview()`: captures `$pageview` on `location.pathname` changes via `useLocation()`
- `usePostHogIdentify()`: subscribes to `useAuthStore`, calls `posthog.identify()` / `posthog.reset()`
- Both are no-ops when `posthogEnabled` is false

### 3. `web/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx` — React error boundary

- Class component, `componentDidCatch` → `posthog.capture('$exception', ...)`
- Fallback UI using Mantine components (title, message, reload button)

### 4. `web/src/lib/posthogExchange.ts` — urql exchange

- Inspects operation results for `graphQLErrors`
- Captures `graphql_error` events with operation name + error messages
- Passthrough when PostHog is disabled

## Files to modify

### 5. `web/src/main.tsx` — Provider + error boundary wiring

```
StrictMode > UrqlProvider > AppProviders > PostHogProvider > ErrorBoundary > BrowserRouter > App
```

### 6. `web/src/App.tsx` — Wire hooks

- Call `usePostHogPageview()` and `usePostHogIdentify()` in App component

### 7. `web/src/lib/urqlClient.ts` — Add exchange

- Insert `posthogExchange` into exchanges: `[cacheExchange, posthogExchange, authExchange(...), fetchExchange]`

### 8. `web/src/pages/Search.tsx` — Custom analytics events

- `search_executed` — on search results load (filter count, result count, has_location)
- `result_clicked` — on institution click (unit_id, name)
- `export_csv` / `export_pdf` — on export button clicks

### 9. `.env.example` — Document new env vars

### 10. `web/src/test/setup.ts` — Global mocks

- Mock `posthog-js` and `posthog-js/react` with no-op stubs

## PostHog init config

```typescript
posthog.init(key, {
  api_host: host || 'https://us.i.posthog.com',
  capture_pageview: false,       // manual via hook
  capture_pageleave: true,
  autocapture: false,            // explicit events only
  respect_dnt: true,
  persistence: 'localStorage',
  loaded: (ph) => { if (import.meta.env.DEV) ph.debug() },
})
```

## Issues (kapow-d1i)

| Issue | Title | Deps | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| `kapow-d1i.1` | PostHog client module + test mocks | — | P1 |
| `kapow-d1i.2` | React error boundary + PostHog exceptions | .1 | P1 |
| `kapow-d1i.3` | GraphQL error tracking exchange | .1 | P2 |
| `kapow-d1i.4` | Pageview + user identify hooks | .1 | P1 |
| `kapow-d1i.5` | Search page analytics events | .1 | P2 |
| `kapow-d1i.6` | PostHog provider wiring | .2, .3, .4, .5 | P1 |

**Parallelism**: After `.1` completes, `.2`–`.5` can all run in parallel. `.6` is the final assembly.

## What this intentionally skips

- **Session replay** — enable later by removing `disable_session_recording: true`
- **Feature flags / experiments** — work automatically once enabled in PostHog dashboard; `PostHogProvider` is already in the tree
- **Server-side tracking** — separate concern for the Go API
- **Cookie consent** — US-focused tool; `respect_dnt: true` is sufficient

# PostHog Client Module + Test Mocks (kapow-d1i.1)

## Context
Kapow needs analytics/error tracking. This issue sets up the PostHog JS client module as the foundation for the full integration (kapow-d1i epic).

## Steps

### 1. Install posthog-js
```bash
cd web && pnpm add posthog-js
```

### 2. Create `web/src/lib/posthog.ts`
Follow the `supabase.ts` pattern:
- Read `VITE_POSTHOG_KEY` and `VITE_POSTHOG_HOST` from `import.meta.env`
- Export `posthogEnabled: boolean` (true when key is present)
- If enabled: call `posthog.init()` with config from plan (capture_pageview: false, autocapture: false, respect_dnt: true, persistence: 'localStorage', etc.)
- If disabled: log a console.info and export `posthog` as-is (posthog-js already no-ops before init)
- Export the posthog instance

### 3. Add global mocks in `web/src/test/setup.ts`
Mock both `posthog-js` and `posthog-js/react` with vi.mock():
- `posthog-js`: init, capture, identify, reset, debug, register, opt_out_capturing — all vi.fn()
- `posthog-js/react`: PostHogProvider (passthrough), usePostHog (returns mock)

### 4. Document env vars in `.env.example`
Add `VITE_POSTHOG_KEY` and `VITE_POSTHOG_HOST` with comments under a new PostHog section.

### 5. Write unit tests `web/src/lib/posthog.test.ts`
Mirror `supabase.test.ts` pattern with vi.resetModules + vi.stubEnv:
- Test: exports posthogEnabled=false and doesn't call init when key missing
- Test: exports posthogEnabled=true and calls init with correct config when key present
- Test: uses default host when VITE_POSTHOG_HOST is absent
- Test: uses custom host when VITE_POSTHOG_HOST is set

### 6. Quality gate
```bash
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test
```

## Key files
- Pattern to follow: `web/src/lib/supabase.ts`, `web/src/lib/supabase.test.ts`
- Test setup: `web/src/test/setup.ts`
- Env docs: `.env.example`

## Verification
- `pnpm build` passes (posthog-js types resolve)
- `pnpm lint` passes (zero warnings)
- `pnpm test` passes (new tests + existing tests unaffected by mocks)

# ─── Kapow API — Environment Variables ────────────────────────────────────────
# Copy this file to .env and fill in real values.
# NEVER commit .env to version control.

# ─── Server ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HOST=0.0.0.0
PORT=8081

# ─── Database ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PostgreSQL connection string (pgx format)
# Required — server will not start without this.
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/kapow?sslmode=disable
# For `make dev-db-up` (standalone dev database on port 5433):
# DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable

# ─── Authentication (JWT) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# HS256 shared secret — used when JWT_PUBLIC_KEY is not set (dev / simple clients)
JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret

# RS256 PEM-encoded public key — if set, overrides JWT_SECRET
# JWT_PUBLIC_KEY=-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

# Expected "iss" claim in incoming tokens (optional)
# JWT_ISSUER=https://auth.example.com

# Expected "aud" claims — comma-separated (optional)
# JWT_AUDIENCE=kapow-api

# ── Supabase shortcut ──
# Use the JWT secret from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API → JWT Secret:
# JWT_SECRET=<your-supabase-jwt-secret>
# JWT_ISSUER=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1
# JWT_AUDIENCE=authenticated

# Future: OIDC discovery URL (not yet active)
# OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL=https://auth.example.com/.well-known/openid-configuration

# ─── Supabase (Frontend Auth SDK) ───────────────────────────────────────────
# Project URL and anon key from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API.
# These are used by the Supabase JS SDK in the frontend for login/signup flows.
# SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
# SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...

# Vite exposes these to the browser (VITE_ prefix required)
# VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
# VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...

# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LOG_LEVEL=info          # trace | debug | info | warn | error
LOG_FORMAT=json         # json (default/prod) | text (dev)

# ─── HTTP Debug Logging ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Request header logging: none (off) | selected | all
# HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS=none
# Comma-separated headers to log when mode is "selected"
# HTTP_DEBUG_SELECTED_HEADERS=Content-Type,Accept,User-Agent
# Redact sensitive headers (Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, X-Api-Key)
# HTTP_DEBUG_REDACT_SENSITIVE=true
# Response logging: none (off) | headers | full (headers + body)
# HTTP_DEBUG_RESPONSE_MODE=none
# Max response body bytes to capture when mode is "full"
# HTTP_DEBUG_BODY_MAX_BYTES=4096

# ─── Environment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ENV=development         # development | staging | production

# ─── CORS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Comma-separated allowed origins (empty = no CORS headers set)
CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:8080

# ─── GraphQL ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND=true     # Enable Playground UI (dev only — disable in prod)
GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION=true  # Enable schema introspection

# ─── MCP (Model Context Protocol) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
MCP_ENABLED=true               # Enable MCP server endpoint at /mcp
